name: Shepherd's Pie
tagline: Spiced lamb mince under a cloud of buttery mashed potato

hero.alt: Shepherd's pie with golden mash topping and bubbling gravy edges
hero.caption: shepherd's pie — lamb under mash

steps.title: Make the mash
steps.detail: Boil potatoes in salted water until fork-tender, ~15 minutes. Drain, return to pot to dry, then mash with warm milk and butter. Season generously.
steps.tip: Pass through a ricer for cloud-like mash. A masher leaves lumps that brown unevenly.

steps.title: Brown the lamb
steps.detail: Brown lamb in a wide skillet over high heat, breaking it up. Drain off excess fat. Add onion and carrot, cook 5 minutes until softening.
steps.tip: Lamb fat is strong; pour off all but 1 tbsp before adding aromatics.

steps.title: Build the gravy
steps.detail: Stir in tomato paste, Worcestershire, then stock. Simmer 8 minutes until syrupy. Stir in peas. Spread into a baking dish.
steps.tip: The filling should look saucy, not soupy — it'll thicken further in the oven.

steps.title: Top and bake
steps.detail: Spread mash over the lamb to seal the edges. Fork the surface for ridges that catch color. Bake at 400°F for 25 minutes until golden.
steps.tip: A 30-second broil at the end deepens the peaks of the mash.

healthFacts: Naturally nut-free — lamb, root veg, and dairy mash, no nuts anywhere.
Shepherd's pie is lamb; cottage pie is beef. The names matter.
Mashing in warm milk and butter gives a fluffier mash — cold dairy seizes the starch.
